title: NOC 94106 – Machining tool operators | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s manufacturing sector is defined by high-precision engineering. Machining Tool Operators (NOC 94106) are the technical experts who bridge the gap between raw metal and the intricate components required for aerospace, medical devices, and robotics. As industry 4.0 matures, these operators are increasingly working with Multi-axis CNC (Computer Numerical Control) systems. For international workers, this NOC is one of the most reliable pathways into the Canadian labor market due to a chronic shortage of specialized machine operators in Ontario, British Columbia, and Manitoba.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group941 – Machine operators in mineral and metal processing

common_job_titles: CNC (Computer Numerical Control) Machining Tool Operator
duties: Machine Setup: Preparing and adjusting various machining tools to perform repetitive operations (drilling, milling, turning, grinding).
Operation: Monitoring machines during operation to ensure they are cutting, boring, or grinding metal to exact engineering tolerances.
Precision Measurement: Using micrometers, calipers, and digital gauges to verify that finished parts meet blueprints.
Maintenance: Performing minor repairs and routine maintenance such as changing tool bits or clearing metal shavings.
2026 Update: Utilizing digital twin software to simulate tool paths before starting a production run to minimize material waste.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is required.
Experience: Previous experience as a labourer or helper in a machine shop is often preferred.
Training: Several weeks of on-the-job training are provided; however, a College Certificate in CNC Machining significantly improves PR chances and starting pay.
Language: CLB 4 or 5 is sufficient for entry-level, but higher proficiency is needed for programming-focused roles.
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) indicates a "Balanced" market nationally, but "Shortages" in high-tech manufacturing corridors. Over 15,000 new job openings are expected due to retirement and the reshoring of precision manufacturing to Canada.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook for machining tool operators is rated as Good to Very Good in Canada’s technological hubs:
Ontario (Kitchener-Waterloo & GTA): Very High demand. This region is a global leader in advanced manufacturing and robotics, facing a severe shortage of CNC operators.
Manitoba (Winnipeg): Good prospects. Driven by a massive aerospace manufacturing sector and agricultural machinery production.
British Columbia: Stable demand. Focused on specialized marine engineering and high-tech equipment manufacturing.
Quebec: Strong demand in the Montreal aerospace corridor and the growing medical technology sector.
Why Work in Canada as a Machining Tool Operator?
Tech-Centric Career: Shift from manual lathes to operating sophisticated 5-axis CNC centers and electrical discharge machines (EDM).
High Wage Growth: Machining is one of the highest-paying TEER 4 categories, with rapid salary increases for those with CNC programming skills.
Path to PR: Frequently featured in PNP In-Demand lists and eligible for the 2026 "Trades" category draws.
Skills Portability: Experience gained in a Canadian high-precision shop is globally recognized, offering long-term career mobility.
pathways: Ontario Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Machining tool operators are a high-priority occupation for provincial nomination in Ontario.
Manitoba Provincial Nominee Program (MPNP): A very successful pathway for those with a job offer in Winnipeg’s manufacturing sector.
Express Entry (Trade Category): 2026 draws for the Trades Category have specifically targeted operators with CNC and precision machining experience.
AIP (Atlantic Immigration Program): Excellent for those looking to work in the specialized marine manufacturing hubs of Nova Scotia or New Brunswick.
